def: s.m. See s.v. go or gava. s.m.f. Water; — s.m. Name of a Rishi; — the sacrifice of a cow; — the thunder-bolt; — the sky; heaven, paradise; — the sun; the moon; a member (of the body); — hair (of the body); — s.f. Speech; Sarasvatī, the goddess of speech; — the earth, the world; a mother; — a quarter of the horizon (as east, west, &c.); — the eye; — an arrow; — adj. Got, obtained; — meek, gentle; harmless, inoffensive; simple (e.g. gaʼū brāhmaṇ)
subs: vulg. gavāksh, gawāććh (˚va+ak˚), s.m. 'A bull's-eye'; an air-hole; a loop-hole, a skylight; a window; lattice; — the light admitted by a window
s.m. The killing of a bull or a cow
s.m. (f. -ī), Cow-keeper, cowherd; — ʻa protector of the earth,' a king; — the cowherd Kr̤ishṇa (one of the epithets of Kr̤ishṇ); — a proper name (of men)
s.m. Title of an Upanishad in honour of Kr̤ishṇa
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Having the charge of kine; — protecting the earth; — a cowherd; — a king
s.m. 'The lord of a herd of cows,' a bull
s.m. 'Son of a cow;' a simpleton; a fool
vulg. go-pasu, s.m. A sacrificial cow
vulg. go-pasutā, s.f. The condition of a cow about to be immolated
or gau-thān, s.m. The place where the cattle of a village are assembled; a station for cattle; a cow-pen ( = go-sthān)

adj. & s.m. Ranged or grazed over by cattle; — offering range (or field or scope) for action; within the range (of), accessible, attainable; within the power (of); within sight (of), visible, perceptible; perceived, known; — the range of cattle, pasturage, pasture-ground; range or reach (of the eye, or the understanding, &c.); — the range (or the conjunction or influence) of the planets; — an object of sense (as sound, shape, &c.); — an object of devotion; — perception; information acquired by the perception of the senses
or gau-dān, s.m. The gift of a cow; an offering of a cow
s.m. A cow's tooth; — yellow orpiment; — a white fossil substance (apparently an earthy salt)
s.m. Possession of cows; property in cattle; — a station of cowherds
s.f. 'Dust of the earth'; sunset (the time at which mist seems to rise from the earth); evening, twilight
s.m. Cow's milk; buttermilk; curdled or coagulated milk
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī). Reared on cow's milk; one who is reared on cow's milk, a child; — a sodomite
s.f. A vessel for holding milk, &c.; a milk-pail

s.m., or go-roćanā, s.f. A bright yellow pigment prepared from the urine or the bile of a cow, or vomited in the shape of scibulœ by that animal (it is employed in painting and dyeing, and is of especial use in marking the foreheads of Hindūs with the tilak or sectarial mark; it is also used in medicine as a sedative, tonic, and anthelmintic remedy, &c.)
or gau-sālā, s.f. & m. Cow-shed, cow-house ( = go-śālā)
go-sāṅʼī or go-sāʼīṅ or go-saiṅyā or go-saiyāṅ
s.m. Udder of a cow; — a cluster of blossoms; — a nosegay
s.f. A bunch of grapes; a grape
s.m. A station for cattle; a cow-stall, cow-house, cow-pen (see go-thān)
s.m. The sacrifice of a cow (one of the great sacrifices of the Hindūs in former times; but not permitted in Kali-yug or the present age)
s.m. Master or possessor of cows or of herds; a religious mendicant; — an honorary title affixed to proper names, &c. (see gusāṅʼī)
or gau-śāl, s.m., or go-śālā, s.f. = go-sālā, q.v.

(gosh = gos, gen. of go), s.m. A cow's foot or hoof; — mark or impression of a cow's foot; — puddle or water sufficient to fill a cow's foot-mark; a small puddle; — a measure of capacity (as much as a cow's foot-print would hold)
vulg. goshṭh, s.m. An abode for cattle; cow-house, cow-pen; — any place where animals are kept; — a station of cowherds
s.f. An assembly, a meeting; a society, an association; a confederacy; — family connexions (esp. the dependant or junior branches); — conversation, discourse, dialogue; dispute

s.m. A cow's ear; — a species of deer, Antilope picta (syn. nīl-gāʼo); — a mule; — a kind of serpent; — a span (from the tip of the thumb to that of the ring-finger); — a place of pilgrimage on the Malabar coast (sacred to Śiva); Śiva (as there worshipped)
(for go-karṇī), s.f. The plant Aletris hyacinthoides, and its flower
s.m. The plant Asteracantha longifolia, and Ruellia longifolia, and Tribulus languinosus
s.m. A herd of kine; a multitude of cattle; a cow-station, cow-house; — a village or tract of the Jamna (the country around Vr̤indāvan, the residence of Krishṇ during his youth)
s.m. A measure of distance (being the distance at which the lowing of a cow may be heard)
s.f. The side of a river, or a tank, &c. at which cows drink
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī or -ā), Noxious to kine; — he who or that which kills kine; one who has killed a cow
s.m. 'Cow-heaven'; the heaven of Vishṇu, or of Krishṇa

vulg. gomtī, s.f. Name of a river in Northern India (it falls into the Ganges between Banāras and Gāzīpūr); — name of a Vedic hymn (a prayer or formula to be repeated during expiation for killing a cow); — cow-dung
or gau-mukh, s.m. A kind of musical instrument (a sort of horn or trumpet); — a hole in a wall (of a peculiar shape), made by thieves, &c.; a breach (syn. seṅdh)

or gau-mukhī, s.f. The chasm in the Himālaya mountains through which the Ganges flows (erroneously conceived to be shaped like a cow's mouth); — a cloth bag containing a rosary (the beads of which are counted by the hand thrust inside)
or gau-mutr, s.m. Cow's urine (which has to be imbibed in certain kinds of penance)

or go-medak, s.m. A gem or precious stone brought from the Himālaya and the Indus (described as of four sorts, — white, pale yellow, red, and dark blue); a kind of yellowish or tawny gem, a topaz; — go-meda-sannibha, s.m. Chalcedony or opal
s.m. The offering or sacrifice of a cow
s.m. (lit. 'having a nose like that of an ox'), A large kind of serpent, a boa-constrictor

s.m. lit. 'Nourisher of kine'; name of a celebrated hill in Vrindāvan, about fifteen miles to the west of Mathurā (it is the Parnassus of the Hindūs; and is said to have been lifted up and supported by Krishṇa upon one finger for seven days, to shelter the cowherds from a storm of rain sent by Indra to test Krishṇa's divinity; — hence Krishṇ is called Govardhan-dhar, and Giri-dhar, 'the hill-supporter')
s.m. 'Lord of Govardhan,' a name of Krishṇa

adj. & s.m. Obtaining cattle; finding cattle or cows; — a cow-keeper, chief herdsman, one of the most usual appellations of Krishṇa in that form; — a proper name (of a man); name of a mountain; — govind-rām, s.m. A proper name (of a man)
s.f. The killing a cow (a most heinous crime among Hindūs)
or gavīś (˚va+īśa), s.m. (f. -ā), An owner of kine.

def: s.m.(?) (dialec.) Unclaimed land (on which cattle graze).
s.m. pl. (of غامضة g̠āmiẓat, fr. g̠āmiẓ, q.v.), Obscure or recondite matters, abstruse questions, &c.
more: s. plur. (of غامض) Obscure or difficult or profound (sayings).

def: s.m. (Braj) = gaman, q.v. s.m. Going, act of going; manner of going, gait; going to, approaching; intercourse (with)
subs: (˚na+ag˚), s.m. Coming and going; intercourse; passing and repassing; thoroughfare
To move, go, pass; to have sexual intercourse (with, -ke sāth).

def: v.t. To cause to sing.
more: (causal of گانا) v.a. To cause to sing.

def: s.m. (Dakkhinī) A singer.
= H گوانري गवांरी gawāṅrī, corr. of gaṅwār, and gaṅwārī, qq.v.
= H گوانر गवांर gawāṅr, corr. of gaṅwār, and gaṅwārī, qq.v.
s.m. Name of a race of gipsies in India.
s.f. The plant Dolichos fabœformis; the bean of this plant (it is commonly given to cattle, and is also eaten by the poorer classes; it is reckoned among the upa-dhānya, or inferior kinds of grain)
more: n. f. A coarse grain, generally given to cattle. Guār khāeṅ gaṅvār . Prov. Coarse meal for village louts. guār kī phalī , n. f. Guār bean.
subs: The bean of the Dolichos fabœformis.

def: s.m. A cradle (i.q. gahwāra, q.v.). s.m. A swing; a cradle.
more: s. m. A cradle. See گہواره gahwāra.

def: s.f. A cow-house; cow-station; — a dwelling-house; a family.

s.f. = gawār or guʼār, q.v. s.f. The plant Dolichos fabœformis; the bean of this plant (it is commonly given to cattle, and is also eaten by the poorer classes; it is reckoned among the upa-dhānya, or inferior kinds of grain)
subs: The bean of the Dolichos fabœformis.

def: s.f. A place where cattle are slaughtered, a slaughter-house.
s.m. A kind of mountain ox; — an elk (syn. sāmbar); a deer; a doe.
s.m. A kind of mountain ox; — an elk (syn. sāmbar); a deer; a doe.
more: s. m. An elk, a deer, a doe.
ex: ہم نے گوزن کا شکار کیا اور اس کے سینگ کاٹ لیے۔
ham ne gavazn kā śikār kiyā aur us ke sīng kāṭ liye
We hunted a stag and cut off its antlers.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گوزن (gawazn, “deer; stag”)

def: s.m. Red chalk (see gerū).

ety: prop. غائر g̠āʼir, act. part. of غور 'to enter deeply' (into
def: adj. Deep, profound, abstruse; considerate, thoughtful; powerful (see g̠aur).
more: (part. act. of غور) adj. 1. Powerful. 2. Considerate.

def: s.m. = gaveś, q.v.s.v. go, or gava.
s. The club of Farīdūn.
s. The club of Farīdūn.
n. m. A bolster which supports the back when seated.
s. m. A large pillow or bolster.

ety: intens. n. fr. غوص 'to dive into'
def: s.m. One who dives (in the sea) for pearls.
more: (adj. superl. of غوص) s. m. A diver (for pearls, &c.).

ety: g̠awwāṣ+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Diving for pearls; — the art or business of a diver.
s.f. (Dakh.) = guphā, q.v. s.f. A cave, cavern; den; vault; — a retreat, hiding-place; a thicket
subs: To settle down in a cave or cell, to lead the life of a hermit.

def: adj. Consisting of cattle or cows; — belonging to, or coming from, a cow; — s.m. Five things coming from a cow, viz. milk; butter; cheese; urine; dung.
s. A kind of bread of a long shape resembling a neatʼs tongue.
s.f. pl. of g̠āyat, q.v.), Ends, extremities, &c.
adj. Of or belonging to, or coming from, a cow (i.q. gāwā)
subs: s.m. Ghee made of cow's milk.

def: s.f. The city of Gayā, in Bihār. (It is a celebrated place of pilgrimage among Hindūs, and is said to have been sanctified by the benediction of Vishṇu as a tribute to Gayā the Asur, who was overwhelmed here with rocks by the gods); — name of a river.
more: p. p. from جانا S. गम to go. Gone; went. gayā guzrā, gayā bītā , 1. Past and gone. 2. Worthless; useless. gayā vaqt . Past time. gayā gavāyā , adj. Lost and gone; stolen. gaë darje , adv. At least; at most. gaīk. , v. n. 1. To overlook; pass over. 2. To fail to do; neglect. Apnī jān meṅ gaī nahīṅ karne kā .
Name of a city in Bihār, being a celebrated tīrth or place of pilgrimage.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit गया (gayā).

ety: perf. part. of jānā, and = Prk. गमिदओ; S. गम+इत+कः, rt. गम्
def: part. adj. (f. gaʼī), Gone; past
subs: (f. gaʼī-bītī), or gayā-guẕrā (f. gaʼī-guẕrī) adj. Past and gone; — worthless, useless
(-ko), To allow to pass unnoticed (an offence, &c.); to pass over, to overlook; to connive at; — to fail to do, to neglect.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit गया (gayā).

def: s.m. A song
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī, and -ikā), Singing; — one who sings, a singer.
more: n. m. gāyan , n. f. A singer; songster.

ety: gayā, p.p. of jānā+Prk. आलो = S. आलः
def: s.m. The land of deceased Biswādārs lying unclaimed; — land coming under the management of the Mālguzār after an Asāmī or cultivator deserts his village.
s.m. = gayāl, q.v. s.m. The land of deceased Biswādārs lying unclaimed; — land coming under the management of the Mālguzār after an Asāmī or cultivator deserts his village.
more: s. m. A class of Gayā Brāhmans, who officiate in certain religious ceremonies performed by pilgrims at that place.

def: s.m. An inhabitant (or a native) of Gayā; — a class of Gayā Brāhmaṇs, who officiate in certain religious ceremonies performed by pilgrims at that place (syn. gayā-wāl).
adj. & s.m. Singing; — a singer; — song.
s.m. & f. A public singer.
s.m. An elephant.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑁂𑀦𑁆𑀤𑀼𑀕 (genduga), from Sanskrit कन्दुक (kanduka).

def: s.m. = next, q.v.

s.f. The utmost extent or limit, the extreme limit or point, end, extremity, extreme; — a banner, standard, flag; — adj. & adv. Superlative, consummate, immense, excessive, extreme, uttermost; — excessively, extremely, very; chiefly
more: adj. See اوهک (1, 2, 5). 1. Considerable; extensive; immense; enormous. 2. Deep; uttermost. 3. Consummate; gross; arrant; extraordinary. 4. Inordinate; immoderate.
n. f. See intihā . It is used mostly with the Arabic particle li up to, as min ibtidāë san—lagāyat —from the beginning of the year—to the end of—.
(from غيّ) 1. adj. Very, extremely, chiefly. 2. s. f. The end, extremity, the extreme. 3. A standard, flag. 4. (prop. غايط) Ordure. علم غايت iﻌlm-i-g̠ẖāyat, Mathematicks.

def: s.m. corr. of g̠āʼit̤, q.v.

s.f. Name of a Vedic metre of twenty-four syllables (generally arranged as a triplet of three divisions of eight syllables each); a hymn composed in this metre; — a sacred verse from the Rig-veda to be recited mentally by every Brāhman at his morning and evening devotions (the Gāyatrī verse is personified as a goddess, the wife of Brahmā, and mother of the four Vedas, and also of the first three classes of Hindūs in their capacity of twice-born: there is but one Gāyatrī of the Vedas; but according to the system of the Tāntrikas there are a number of mystical verses called by that name, and each deity has one in particular: the repetition of the Gāyatrī is considered necessary to salvation).
s.m. (f. -iṇī), One who sings hymns; a singer; — the tree Acacia catechu.
more: n. f. A sacred verse from the Rig Veda repeated mentally.
s. f. A prayer repeated by Brāhmaṇs with the rosary.

def: s.m. (f. -iṇī), One who sings hymns; a singer; — the tree Acacia catechu.
n. m. Gaya Brahmans who officiate at the temples there.
s.f. A singer, songstress.
adj. Very jealous (in point of love or honour); high-minded; — an epithet of the Deity.
more: (from غير) adj. 1. Jealous in point of honour or love, high-minded. 2. An epithet of the deity.

ety: gayūr, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Jealousy ( = g̠airat).

ety: Pehl. vazr; Zend vazra, 'club,' rt. vaz; S. वज्र
def: s.m. An iron bar; a ramrod, a rammer; bow (of a fiddle); — an instrument for measuring with; a yard measure; a foot-rule; a yard (of measure, varying formerly from about thirty-two English inches upwards; and now fixed by Government at thirty-three inches)
more: n. m. 1. An iron bar. 2. A ramrod; rammer. Bandūq kā gaz . 3. The bow of a fiddle. 4. A yard measure. 5. A foot rule. gaz bhar . A yard (of any thing). gaz se gaz . A square yard. gaz k. , v. a. To ram (a gun). desī gaz , The native yard, 33 inches long. lambarī gaz , n. m. The Govt. yard of 36 inches.
s. m. 1. An instrument for measuring with, a yard, an ell. 2. A ramrod.
subs: A full yard; a yard (of anything)
s.f. Measuring with a gaz; — gaz-gat karnā, To measure with a gaz
or desī gaz, The yard of thirty-three inches

def: s.m. Rouge for the face; perfumed powder for the hair or skin (cf. upṭan)
more: n. m. Perfumed powder for the hair or skin.
s. m. Red colour with which women paint the face: see اپٿن or غازه کاري .اپٿنا g̠ẖāza-kārī, s. f. Painting the face with a red colour.
subs: s.f. Applying rouge to the face, &c.

ety: inf. n. of غزو 'to go forth to war'
def: s.m. Waging war, going forth to war (esp. against infldels)
more: s. m. Making war (against infidels). غزاصفا g̠ẖazā ṣafā, War, plunder, devastation.

ety: imperf. part. of gazīdan = prep. ga + zīdan = zadan; — Zend vi+rt. jan; S. वि+हन् (हन्ति)
def: part. adj. Biting; stinging; injuring, hurting; hurtful; — s.m. Injury, hurt; loss, damage.
more: 1. part. act. (of گزيدن) Biting, hurting. 2. s. Loss, hurt.

ety: inf. n. of غضب 'to be angry'
def: s.m. Anger, passion, wrath, rage, fury, vengeance; wrong, impropriety, injustice, oppression, outrage; violence, force, compulsion; — a curse, calamity, affliction, woe, a fearful thing, an awful event; — adj. & s.m. Angry, wrathful, offended, &c.; fearless, daring; outrageous; great, excessive, intense, tremendous, awful, fearful, dreadful, shocking; woeful, calamitous, injurious, hurtful, detrimental (to, -ke ḥaq-meṅ); — too good; very fine, splendid, dazzling, very beautiful; wonderful, rare, extraordinary, unique; — an oppressor, &c. ( = g̠aẓabī); a fearless man, a dare-devil
more: adj. غضب was angry. 1. Angry; offended; displeased. 2. Too good; too bad; excessive; dreadful; shocking. Gazab garmī paṛtī hai . 3. Gigantic; stupendous; splendid; dazzling. Āj to gazab ban-ke nikle ho! 4. Very beautiful; angelic. Ham jis pe haiṅ āshaq, woh parī-zād gazab hai . Zauq. 5. Unique ( achambā , and anokhā 1-5). Uskī jo bāt hai, woh gazab hai . 6. Destructive; hurtful; baneful. Yeh bad-mizājī tumhāre haq meṅ gazab hai .

n. m. Vengeance; wrath; woe (اَفت). 1. Buṛhyā gazab kī puṛyā . Prov. An old woman is a packet of evil (Pandora's box). 2. Burā hākim Khudā kā gazab . Prov. A bad ruler is an instrument of God's vengeance. 3. Yeh kyā thoṛā gazab hai ki Hindustān ko kaṅgāl kar dīyā? Is the impoverishment of India a small calamity? 4. Dārūë gazab khamoshī . Prov. Wrath's cure is silence. 5. Khudā ke gazab se zarā dil meṅ kāṅp, Chugal-khor ke muṅh ko ḍaste haiṅ sāṅp . Mīr Hasan. With fear of wrath divine your heart should tremble just, The wicked tongues' of tale-bearers the snake bite must. gazab-i-ilahī, yā /?/khudā . Divine wrath, or gazab toṛnā, yā ḍhānā , Wom. v. a. To wreak one's vengeance on. Mue gazbī ne gazab ḍhā rakkhā hai . Mah. Wom. gazab ṭūṭnā , v. n. See āfat ānā , 1. gazab k. , v. a. 1. (Rus. gajab ghālnā ) To do or work wonders; to surprise. Bolte tum to kyā gazab karte, Sau sitam karte ho, b…

1. s. m. Violence, oppression, injustice, compulsion, passion, rage, fury, wrath, vengeance, anger, curse, calamity. 2. adj. Angry. غضب آلوده g̠ẖaẓab-ālūda, adj. Oppressive. غضب کرنا g̠ẖaẓab karnā, To oppress, to tyrannise (over). غضبناک g̠ẖaẓab-nāk, adj. Enraged, indignant, wrathful. غضبناکي g̠ẖaẓab-nāk-ī, s. f. Indignation.
subs: adj. Full of rage, enraged, wrathful, indignant; oppressive, &c.
or g̠aẓab ṭūṭnā (-par), To be visited by (the) wrath (of God), to be overtaken by calamity or affliction
or g̠aẓab ḍhānā (-par), To visit (one) with wrath, to wreak vengeance (on); to practise cruel oppression (on), &c.
'The wrath of God'; — have some fear of God's wrath! good gracious! how shocking! how fearful! (a phrase used when one sees or hears another saying or doing something outrageous or revolting)

(rustic, gajab ghālnā), v.n. To be angry or wrathful (with, -par), be enraged or indignant (at); to practise oppression (on), to oppress, to tyrannize (over); to commit an impropriety, or an outrage; to work or do harm or mischief; to do anything fearful or awful; — to do something wonderful or surprising, to work wonders
adj. Full of rage, enraged, indignant, passionate, furious
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غضب (ġazab), from Arabic غَضَب (ḡaḍab).

def: adj. & s.m. (H. fem. g̠aẓaban), Angry, wrathful, passionate, furious; — fearless, &c. (see g̠aẓab); — a passionate, or a violent man; an oppressor; a fearless man, a dare-devil, a terrible fellow; a firebrand; — s.f. Anger, wrath, &c. ( = g̠aẓab).
more: n. m. gazban, gajban , n. f. Wom. An oppressor; a firebrand. 1. Baṛā gazbī marduā hai . Wom. 2. Yo ke kām kare sai gajban! sai yo kām burā, kāman . W. Rus.

ety: gaz˚, prob. fr. gazīdan+dim. aff. ak
def: s.f. A relish (as fruit, or sweets, taken with wine, or after opium, &c.); a sweetmeat made of sesamum and sugar.
more: n. f. 1. Relish (taken after wine, opium, etc.). 1. Kaho to mohaiyā ho bazm-i-sharāb. Maṅgāëṅ gazak ke liye bhī kabāb! Shauq. 2. Afyūnī kī gazak miṭhāī, Sharābī kī gazak namkīnī . Prov. For opium-eaters sweets, for wine-bibbers salted food. 2. A sweetmeat of sesamum and sugar. 1. Gajak khoprā jāṛe kā! Delhi Street cry. 2. Gazak, paṭṭī, halvāson jāṛe kā! Delhi Street cry.
s. f. A relish (eaten with wine, &c.), a devil.

ety: inf. n. of غزل 'to talk, &c. in an amatory and enticing manner'
def: s.f. An amatory poem, an ode
more: n. f. pl. gazalyāt . An ode; an amatory or Bacchanalian poem. gazal-khuāṅ . A reciter of odes. gazal-khuānī , n. f. The reciting of odes. gazal kahnā , v. n. To compose an ode.

s. f. An ode, an amatory poem. (Plur. غزليات). غزل خوان g̠ẖazal-ḵẖẉān, A reciter of odes. غزل خواني g̠ẖazal-ḵẖẉān-ī, s. f. The reciting of odes. غزل سرا g̠ẖazal-sarā, adj. Singing an ode. غزل سراي g̠ẖazal-sarā-ī, s. f. Singing of odes.
subs: adj. & s.m. Singing, or singer of, an ode
s.f. The singing of an ode or odes
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَزَل (ġazal), from Arabic غَزَل (ḡazal).

def: s.m. A young gazelle or deer, a fawn; — the sun; — a delicate young person.
more: (improp. g̠ẖizāl ), s. m. 1. A young deer, a fawn. 2. The sun. 3. A delicate young person. (Pers. plur. غزالان).

ety: for A. غزالة, fem. of g̠azāl
def: s.m. A young gazelle; a young deer, a fawn.
more: (improp. g̠ẖizāla ) s. m. A young deer, a fawn.

def: part. adj. = gazā, q.v. s.m. Rouge for the face; perfumed powder for the hair or skin (cf. upṭan)

subs: (or ghāṭī) karnā (-kī), To raise the uvula (of a child, with the fingers, — a practice of nurses in certain diseases, &c.).

def: s.f. A small bell; — a small metal pot.
wety: Related to گھنٹہ (ghanṭa, “hour”).

def: n. f. 1. A small bell. 2. A small metal pot.
s.f. (Dakh.) A knot; — strips of different coloured cloths tied round the ankles by Moḥarram faqīrs.
s.f. The uvula or soft palate.
s.m. A string of bells (such as is tied on an animal's neck by way of ornament).

ety: prob. fr. S. घातः and ग्रभ्; cf. gapaknā; and gap-ā-gap
def: adj. & s.m. Addicted to swallowing, or bolting, or consuming, &c.; living from hand to mouth; squandering; improvident, &c.; — a spendthrift; a squanderer; one who makes away with (property, &c.), one who embezzles (cf. gāʼo-ghap).
Vox congressus in coitu.
adj. (dakh.) Silent, mute.
s.f. = khapćī or khapaććī, q.v.
more: s. f. The grasp of the two arms.

ety: ghap˚, prob. = khap˚, see khapnā+Prk. अलओ = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. Confusion, jumble, mess; error, discrepancy (in an account); complication; — entanglement; — deception, duplicity, fraud; — an unlawful attachment, a liaison
more: n. m. 1. See السيتَ (2-4). 2. Confusion; mess. ghaplā paṛnā , v. n. To occur (a mistake in accounts). ghaplā ḍālnā , v. a. To make a mess of; to confuse.
subs: (-meṅ), To confuse, jumble, make a mess (of), &c.

def: s.m. Land worn away by running water; a ravine; clay soil in low situations (where rain-water lies for a time).

s.f. = ghāṭ, 'throat,' q.v. s.f. Proper or suitable time or period (for a work or action), opportune moment, opportunity; — design, intention, aim; — cover, concealment, ambush, ambuscade, trap, snare; enmity, treachery
subs: (-kī), or ghāt-meṅ baiṭhnā or rahnā (-kī), To watch for an opportunity (against); to lie in wait or ambush (for); to waylay

def: s.m. House, dwelling, mansion, habitation, abode, home; — a room (in a house), an apartment; — a den; nest; hole; sty; cell; burrow; — a receptacle; frame; stand; cup; pan; case; holder; socket; groove; channel; — a drawer; till; pigeon-hole (of a desk, &c.); a compartment or division; partition; — room, space; — a square or cell (of a chess-board, almanac, &c.); a loop, loop-hole; button-hole; — station, seat, region, quarter; — source, spring, mine, root, parent; place of origination or production, native place
more: n. m. S. गृह Pālī, geham, geho , Pr. ghara . 1. A house ( khānah ). 2. A dwelling; habitation; mansion. 1. Ghar meṅ nahīṅ būr, beṭā māṅge motī-chūr . Prov. Not even bran in the house, and the son and heir wants lollypops! 2. Bāhar miyāṅ sūbedār, ghar meṅ bīvī jhokeṅ bhāṛ . Prov. My lord's abroad a captain gay, his wife at home the fire feeds all the day. 3. Ghar āë kutte ko bhī nahīṅ mārte haiṅ . Prov. We do not drive away even a dog that comes to our house (for refuge). 4. Āṭe kā chirāg ghar rakkhūṅ to chūhā khāë, bāhar rakkhūṅ to kavvā le jāë . Prov. A corn-flour lamp inside the house by rats will eaten be, keep it outside by crows it's snapped up speedily. 5. Jo yārī jorego, chhorā, do ek dinā ko tau nā tyāgūṅgī ghar ko balmā, Jo yārī jorego, chhorā, sadā madā ko tyāg dūṅgī ghar ko balmā, Yārī joṛego to rākhūṅgī gale ko ḍhulnā . Rus. Wom. song. If you 'll but be my love, boy! Just for…

n. m. Cont. of گهورَا A horse. ghuṛ-bahal , n. m. A vehicle drawn by a horse. ghuṛ-chaṛhā , n. m. 1. A mounted soldier (Sikhs). 2. A horseman; trooper. ghuṛ-chaṛhī , n. f. 1. The bridegroom riding on horseback. This ceremony is observed on the way to the bride's house with the nuptial procession. 2. Cavalry; field battery. ghuṛ-dauṛ , n. f. 1. A horse race. 2. A race-course. ghuṛ-dauṛ kā ghoṛā , n. m. A race horse. ghuṛ-sāl , A. astabal , n. m. A stable. ghuṛ-sem , n. f. The horse bean. ghuṛ-nāl , A cannon. ghur-makkhī , n. f. A horse-fly. ghuṛ-muṅhā , adj. Horse-faced.

s. m. House, dwelling, home, apartment, drawer, compartment, groove. گهربسا ghar-basā, 1. At home. 2. Indolent, inactive. گهربگهر ghar-ba-ghar, adj. To or in every house, from house to house. گهربيٿهنا ghar baiṭhnā, To be ruined. Mīr Dard says: جس سمت کوتوني آنکهہ اٿهاکر ديکها مانند حباب گهرکي گهربيٿهہ گئي Jis simt ko tū ne ānkh uṭhākar dekhā, mānand-i-ḥubāb ghar ke ghar baiṭh gaʼe, Wherever you have turned your eyes, whole families, like bubbles of water, have vanished. گهرجانا ghar jānā, To be ruined. گهرچلانا ghar chalānā, To provide for oneʼs household expenses. (Beng.). گهردار ghar-dār, s. m. (dakh.) Household, housekeeping. گهردواري ghar-dwārī, s. f. A kind of tax, poll-tax, hearth-money. گهرڐبونا ghar ḍubonā, To ruin oneʼs self and family. گهرڐوبنا ghar ḍūbnā, To be ruined. گهر کا دهيلانا رهنا ghar kā dhelānā rahnā (dakh. i. q. ويران هونا) To become, or to be, desolate. گهر کرنا g…
subs: To take a wife; to build up a house or family, to beget children; to make a home prosperous, or cheerful, &c.
s.m. House and home; a house and premises; dwelling-place; — family; household; — household goods
or ghar-bār basnā, A house to be inhabited; — to have a wife, to be married
To take a wife; to consummate a marriage; — ghar-bār-kī honā, To be the lady of a house
s.m. A married and house-keeping man; a householder; a family man; — s.f. Housewifery, domestic concerns, family affairs
s.m. One who confines himself to the house, a stay-at-home; a house-bird, home-bird; a moll-coddle; — adj. Stay-at-home; inactive; indolent
To bring ruin on a house
v.n. A house to be ruined; — a wife (or husband) to die
adv. To (or in) every house; from house to house ( = ghar-ghar)

To build a house; to build up (one's) house, make a home (for oneself); to fix, settle, or establish a family; to settle (oneself); to make (oneself) comfortable; to feather (one's) nest; — to manage a house well ( = ghar ćalānā)
s.f. A slave born in the house
To sit at home; to be out of employment
(-kā), A house to fall in, or to come down; a family (or house) to be ruined
adv. Seated at home; — at one's ease; — ghar-baiṭhe-kī naukrī or tanḵẖẉāh, A sinecure
s.f. A kind of house-tax (now abolished)
'To set (one's) house on fire and look on at the sport'; to waste one's substance in idle pleasures
v.n. To go home; — a house (or family) to disappear or be ruined
s.f. The homestead; the proprietor's own cultivation
s.f. A woman who goes about to and from the houses of her female neighbours; a gad-about
(apnā), To keep (one's) house a-going; to provide the expenses for one's household; — to manage a house well
s.m. Dwelling-place, house; house and home
s.f. A tax (formerly) levied from householders and shopkeepers; poll-tax; hearth-money
'To sink the household,' to ruin oneself and family
v.n. House (or family) to be ruined
s.f. An advance of money (to a peasant, &c.) to enable him to build a house for himself
(or ghar-ko) sir-par uṭhānā, To take upon oneself the management of a house; — to raise the household, to create a disturbance in a house; to make a terrible noise or commotion
(-ko), To deprive (one) of house and home; to turn (one) out of doors
To stick in the house, to keep much at home (cf. ghar-basā)
adj. (f. -kī), Of or belonging to the house, family, household, domestic; — one's own; private; familiar
A house to be completely ruined
s.m. One who knows the secrets of a house; — a confidant
s.m. The whole family or household
s.m. A hero in his own house (or at home)
To bring disgrace on the family

To build a house; — to fix, settle, or establish a family; to have a family; to take a wife; — to make an abode or a home (in, — meṅ), take up (one's) abode (in the heart of); — to make (oneself) at home, to be on a familiar footing (with)
'To ruin (one's) home and look on at the sport,' to ruin oneself and spend the time in idle pleasures
To be quits, be even, be as before
or ghar-log, or ghar-ke ādmī, s.m. pl. The house-folk; the family; — the wife
adv. To (or in) every house; from house to house; at every house
or ghar-basā, s.m. One who ruins or destroys a house (see ghar jānā)
To find place or room in (one's) house; to be lodged or kept in the house (of, -ke); to become the kept mistress (of)
(-ko), To keep (a woman as a mistress)
s.m. (f. -ī), Housekeeper, householder; master of a house; husband; — occupant or inmate of a house
s.f. Mistress of a house; wife; — housekeeping
(-se), A happy or comfortable home to be made or formed (by).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀖𑀭 (ghara, a metathesized form of Sanskrit गृह (gṛha)), from Proto-Middle Indo-Aryan *𑀕𑀭𑁆𑀳 (*garha), from Sanskrit गृह (gṛha), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *gʰr̥dʰás, from Proto-Indo-European *gʰerdʰ-.

adj. (dakh.) Thick, close (as branches). s. Thicket (?).
s.f. Workmanship (of jewellery, &c.); — cost of having (jewels, &c.) made.
s. plur. (of غريب) Strange, wonderful (things).
adj. (f. -ī), Domestic; domesticated, tame; home-bred; home-made.

def: s.m. Family, household; house, family, race, lineage (syn. ḵẖāndān).
more: n. m. See خاندان gharātī , Opp. of bārātī , n. m. The bride's relations.

v. a. Caus. of گهرَنا q. v. 1. Rāmnagar meṅ gahnā gaṛhavloṅ, Arrah meṅ le gaile chor, Je Rām dehalin, se hī Rām lehalin, kachhu na bigṛal mor . Bhoj. Wom. song. Some jewels in Rāmnagar made, in Arrah light fingers came by, Ram gave and Ram took away, in nothing the poorer am I. 2. Ghaṛvāyā hai . I've ordered one like it for you. (Dont you wish you may get it?).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀖𑀭𑀸𑀬𑀬𑀡 (*gharāyayaṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-) from Sanskrit *घरायतन (*gharāyatana), from *घर (*ghara) + आयतन (āyátana), in which *घर (*ghara) is a reborrowed Prakrit form of गृह (gṛhá) (or गृध (gṛdha), which is from Proto-Indo-Iranian *gʰr̥dʰás, from Proto-Indo-European *gʰr̥dʰós, from *gʰerdʰ- (“to enclose”). Cognate with Punjabi ਘਰਾਨਾ (gharānā) / گھرانہ (ghrānh), S…

ety: caus. of ghaṛnā; — ghaṛā = ghaṛāw˚ = Prk. घडावे˚ = S. घट+आपि caus. aug.
def: v.t. To cause to form, or fashion, &c. (see ghaṛnā); — to cause to be made, or forged, or fashioned, &c.; to have (a thing) made or fashioned, &c. ( = ghaṛwānā).
s.f. = ghaṛat, q.v.
(dakh.) In a crack, suddenly. غڙپ کرنا g̠ẖaṛap karnā, v. a. To drown, to immerse. غڙپ هونا g̠ẖaṛap honā, v. n. To be drowned or immersed.
s.m. Dry moṭh, or gram, cut and given as fodder to cattle.
n. m. Fodder of gram.
s. (dakh.) Gargle ( Harris ): see غرغره g̠ẖarg̠ẖara.

s.f. corr. of garārī, q.v. s.f. An instrument for twisting thread or string, a reel; the pulley or block over which a well-rope passes; (loc. or dialec.) a groove, a rut; the groove or rut in which moves the wheel of a mill for preparing mortar, &c.
s.f. = ghaṛat, q.v.
more: n. f. See بناوتَ 2, 3, 6, 8. Ghaṛat ke tum bhī ustād ho .

ety: ghaṛ(nā)+at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्ति (āpi, caus. aug.+suff. tī), cf. ghaṛant
def: s.f. Making, fashioning; beating, hammering; forging; — make, form, fashion, shape; — workmanship; — fabrication, invention (cf. gaṛhat; — syn. banāwaṭ).
adj. Of or belonging to a house or family; household, domestic.
n. f. H. ghaṛā pot, ūṅchā high. A wooden stand for water pots, etc.
s.m. A small house (made by children to play in); a toy-house; — adj. = gharaʼū, q.v.
s.f. = ghaṛāʼī, q.v. s.f. Workmanship (of jewellery, &c.); — cost of having (jewels, &c.) made.
v.t. = ghaṛānā, q.v. s.m. Family, household; house, family, race, lineage (syn. ḵẖāndān).
s. m. Family, household goods. گهربار بسانا ghar-bār basānā, To consummate a marriage. گهر بارهونا ghar-bār honā, To undergo the consummation of a marriage.
s. m. Household furniture, &c.
s. f. Housewifery, domestick concerns.
